data.name: Product Manager II
data.jobDescription:
Overview

Microsoft 365 Copilot is a new offering that harnesses the power of generative AI through foundational large language models to usher in a new era of AI-first productivity for hundreds of millions of enterprise users. As business workflows don't typically run exclusively on Microsoft 365 applications and data formats, Copilot is built as a platform right from the very beginning. Using extensibility, customers augment M365 Copilot with custom skills and organizational-specific knowledge to enable truly spectacular AI scenarios and power connected workflows.

As a Product Manager II for Store Operations on the M365 Copilot Platform team, you will drive operational excellence in agent publishing, new platform feature readiness for agents, co-create customer and developer experience & empower developer partners to deliver high-quality Copilot agents. Your work will sit at the intersection of generative AI, platform extensibility, and developer experience, directly impacting productivity for millions of enterprise users and supporting organizations aspiring to become Frontier Firms.

In this role, you will lead initiatives to define and uphold quality standards, streamline agent validation, define readiness for new platform features for high quality agents. You will influence product strategy and roadmap priorities, leveraging data-driven insights and market feedback to improve both developer and user experiences.

Success in this role requires strong ownership, accountability, bias for action. the ability to influence without authority, and a growth mindset to thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment. You will demonstrate technical depth in AI and cloud services, collaborate across business and technical domains, and champion innovation in agent solutions. We seek an “agent boss”—someone who embraces generative AI, adopts a “learn-it-all” mindset, and leads by example.

We believe that we are rewiring productivity for the next 30 years! If you are passionate about operational excellence, developer empowerment, and the transformative potential of AI, we invite you to help us reimagine productivity for the next generation.

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond.



Responsibilities
  • Lead discussions related to new feature readiness for high quality agents, with platform engineering, Developer advocates, and other internal stakeholders for alignments.
  • Lead agent quality initiatives aligned with product goals and industry best practices.
  • Define quality standards, minimum value bar, SLAs, and KPIs for agent store operations at scale.
  • Resolve developer queries and ensure agile, high-quality agent publishing.
  • Partner with engineering to influence roadmap priorities using developer feedback and telemetry.
  • Represent developer and user experience needs in product reviews and planning.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to streamline agent validation and app store readiness.
  • Drive data-driven process improvements for Copilot agent store validation and publisher experience.
  • Develop and release AI-powered tools to enhance operational efficiency and agent quality.
  • Partner with globally distributed teams to release tools that enable developers to easily identify quality issues prior to agent submission.
  • Manage operational escalations and stakeholder communications.
  • Track publishing process and store metrics, aligning stakeholders through regular updates.
  • Gather and synthesize market feedback to shape product strategy and roadmap.
  • Stay updated on AI agent platform trends.
  • Work with strategic customers to define what makes high-quality agents.
  • Demonstrate strong project management, leadership, and cross-group collaboration skills to drive critical alignments for releases.
  • Engage in technical discussions, especially in generative AI and LLM domains to contribute to improvements in the platform experience.
  • Embody Microsoft’s culture and values, fostering a diverse and inclusive environment.


Qualifications

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's Degree AND 2+ years experience in product/service/program management or software development.
    • OR equivalent experience.
  • Proven success in commercial SaaS partnerships and/or customer-facing engagements.
  • Experience with web/mobile technologies, cloud services architecture, and operations at scale.
  • Working knowledge of AI orchestration, agent invocation flows, and AI models.
  • Ability to simplify complex developer workflows and build empathy-driven solutions.
  • Skilled in collecting and presenting developer feedback to influence product and documentation roadmaps.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and executive presence.
  • Ability to work across business, product, design, and technical domains.
  • Demonstrated product thinking, growth mindset, and user experience critique.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Data-driven approach to product decisions and growth.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • 6+ years of product development experience with developer partners and customers.
  • Experience in generative AI solutions and awareness of the evolving AI landscape.
  • Nice to have: Hands-on expertise with Microsoft technology stack (M365 Copilot extensibility, AI agents, Azure OpenAI, Graph Connectors, Teams extensibility).
  • Experience with low-code/no-code AI agent tools.
  • Strong curiosity and ability to thrive in ambiguous, rapidly changing environments.

 

#Copilot

#M365Copilot

#CopilotExtensibility

#CopilotPlatform

#Agents

#CopilotAgents

#AgentCenter


This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.



Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

blogs.title: Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely ru
blogs.description: As digital sovereignty becomes a strategic requirement, organizations are rethinking how they deploy critical infrastructure and AI capabilities under tighter regulatory expectations and higher risk conditions. Microsoft’s approach to sovereignty is grounded in enabling enterprises, public sectors and regulated industries to participate in the digital economy securely, independently and on their own terms. The Microsoft Sovereign Cloud brings together productivity, security and cloud workloads to span both... The post  Microsoft Sovereign Cloud adds governance, productivity and support for large AI models securely running even when completely disconnected   appeared first on  The Official Microsoft Blog .
